rationale: Flagged positive on the report; sheet notes this pads for white cells in urine (pyuria), suggestive of urinary tract inflammation/infection.

text: Leukocyte esterase positive is an indirect marker for white blood cells in urine (pyuria), pointing toward possible urinary tract inflammation or infection. Classic pairing with nitrites; recommend correlating with microscopic WBC count and nitrite result before concluding infection.
rationale: Synthesized from the Internal Notes column for Leukocyte Esterase, which flags it as an indirect enzyme marker and notes the classic pairing with nitrites.

text: This is a finding your provider interprets, not one you adjust with habits. Support general wellness by staying well hydrated, use a clean-catch technique for any repeat sample, and keep up steady overall wellness routines.
rationale: Taken directly from the 30-Day Plan column for Leukocyte Esterase.
